sentence_reviews.text: Derivatives, gradients, Jacobians, chain rules, and automatic differentiation explain how parameter changes affect objectives and computation graphs.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Baydin et al. abstract lines 3-6 and 23-30, plus lines 33-37; Vassilev et al. abstract states AD applies the chain rule repeatedly to computer programs and supports gradient computation.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources directly support derivatives, gradients, Hessians, chain rule application, computational graphs, and AD on programs. 'Explain how parameter changes affect objectives' is a synthesis of those claims rather than a verbatim statement.

sentence_reviews.text: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Calculus & automatic differentiation theory is modeled as an upstream prerequisite for downstream nodes such as Optimization theory, Numerical analysis, Differential equations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
